untranslatable_reason: Signal requires external macro data (foreign vs domestic interest rate spreads, their percentiles, and rolling statistics) which are not available in the allowed indicator set. The allowed indicators are limited to price action, technical analysis, and volatility metrics derived from a single asset's OHLCV data. Foreign interest rate levels, rate spreads, and their statistical distributions across lookback windows require external economic data feeds not present in the backtesting engine's indicator library.
translation_notes: NATENBERG_DRY_44_0 is a regime-based macro signal for options portfolio adjustment. It requires: (1) real-time foreign and domestic interest rate data, (2) calculation of rate spread in basis points, (3) percentile ranking of this spread within a rolling window. These are not derivable from price/volume data alone and would need to be added as external data sources or custom indicators to the backtesting system. The signal is conceptually sound but cannot be implemented with the current allowed indicator set.
